attacking_style: Not an attacker; primarily a sprint finisher who capitalizes on reduced bunch scenarios after hard, selective stages of 200+ km. Wins come from positioning in chaotic finishes rather than pure speed.
team_tactics: Team provides standard lead-out work but Bonifazio frequently fails to maintain optimal position despite domestique support. Struggles in highly organized sprint finishes, performs better when sprint trains are disrupted.
terrain_preference: Hard, selective stages (200+ km) that reduce peloton size but don't eliminate sprinters entirely. Thrives in chaotic, disorganized finishes where positioning deficits matter less. Exposed in pure flat sprints with organized trains.
vulnerability: Positioning in chaotic sprints is a critical weakness—often found at back of peloton at 2km despite team support. Crashes on technical/narrow roads. Lacks top-5 acceleration when positioned well.
key_patterns: Wins from reduced bunches after selective stages, not pure sprints (2 examples)
Chronically poor positioning despite team lead-out work, found at back within 2km (2 observations)
Performs better when chaos/crashes disrupt organized sprint trains (2 instances)
Top-10 capable but lacks acceleration to convert good position to top-5 (2 examples)
Crash-prone on technical roads during crucial race phases (1 abandonment noted)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2020 win shows capability in selective conditions, but fundamental positioning weaknesses persist across all years observed
